investigation_plan: Repo architecture: Next.js App Router confirmed with (dashboard) and (auth) layouts.
Component system: Found UI primitives in components/ui; identified design drift and hardcoded Tailwind colors.
Data layer: Mongoose singleton connection; identified N+1 query patterns in Search API.
Auth: NextAuth with JWT-based RBAC; identified missing RBAC consistency in Leads module.
Static checks: Failed due to Framer Motion type mismatches and Lint path configuration errors.
Responsiveness: Evaluated AppShell and primary dashboard pages for mobile-first compliance.

findings.user_pain: Inability to deploy or run production builds.
findings.impact: Project is stuck in a non-buildable state.
findings.description: Framer Motion Variant type mismatch. Types of property "ease" are incompatible (string vs Easing).
findings.suggested_fix: Align Framer Motion types or update variants to use valid Easing unions from the library.

findings.user_pain: Flickering UI and potential React state errors on page load.
findings.impact: Degraded perceived performance and potential runtime crashes.
findings.description: Initial state uses `new Date().toISOString()`, causing hydration mismatches between server-render (UTC/Server Time) and client-hydration (Local Time).
findings.reproduction: Refresh Candidates or Requirements page; check console for hydration warnings.
findings.suggested_fix: Move date initialization to `useEffect` or use a stable static string for initial render.

findings.user_pain: Inconsistent data visibility for different roles.
findings.impact: Security risk: potential data leakage or unauthorized access in the Leads module.
findings.description: The Leads module manually implements role checks instead of using the centralized `rbac.ts` utility, missing soft-delete (deletedAt) filters.
findings.suggested_fix: Refactor Leads API to use centralized RBAC helper functions.

findings.user_pain: The UI feels "unfinished" or inconsistent due to mixed design languages.
findings.impact: Poor product brand perception and reduced trust.
findings.description: GlassCard uses hardcoded dark glass styles on a light cream theme. LeadCard uses raw Tailwind colors (e.g., emerald-600) instead of design system tokens (e.g., var(--success)).
findings.reproduction: Navigate to Leads Kanban board; observe color mismatches.
findings.suggested_fix: Standardize all color instances to use CSS variables defined in globals.css.

findings.user_pain: Frustrating form experience; mistakes are only caught after submission.
findings.impact: Increased user error and slower data entry.
findings.description: Forms use manual state checks and simple alerts instead of inline validation, Zod schemas, or React Hook Form.
findings.reproduction: Try submitting an empty Candidate or Requirement form.
findings.suggested_fix: Integrate React Hook Form with the existing Zod schemas in `lib/validators/common.ts`.

findings.user_pain: Global search feels laggy as database grows.
findings.impact: Increased database load and poor UX for power users.
findings.description: Search API performs sequential MongoDB queries for related Company/Requirement data (N+1 issue) and lacks text indexing.
findings.suggested_fix: Use MongoDB `$lookup` or Mongoose `.populate()` and implement a text search index.

findings.user_pain: Keyboard-only users cannot reliably use the dashboard.
findings.impact: WCAG non-compliance; exclusion of users with motor impairments.
findings.description: Rating stars and dropdown triggers lack distinct focus-visible states. Modal focus traps need verification.
findings.reproduction: Attempt to navigate the Candidates cards using only the Tab key.
findings.suggested_fix: Apply "focus-ring" utility to all interactive elements and ensure ARIA labels are active.

findings.impact: Potential security breach if weak secrets are used in production.
findings.description: .env.example contains a weak placeholder secret "secure-random-secret" which might be copied literally into dev environments.
findings.suggested_fix: Update documentation to require `openssl rand -base64 32` for the secret.

exec_summary: Overall: The Magnus Copo project has a strong foundation but suffers from significant "UI Drift" and technical debt in build configurations.
Engineering Goal: Stabilize the build (TSC + Lint) and centralize RBAC logic to prevent data leakage.
UI/UX Goal: Finalize the Design System 2.0 implementation by replacing hardcoded Tailwind colors with semantic CSS tokens.
Performance: Optimize Search and Candidate fetching (move to server components or cache layers) to eliminate layout shifts.
questions_for_human: Should I proceed with fixing the TSC/Lint errors first to stabilize the build?
Confirm if the Leads module should be accessible to RECRUITERS (currently blocked by manual RBAC check).
